wrote down all three dates on their slates, and then added them up, and
reduced the answer to shillings and pence.

вҖңTake off your hat,вҖқ the King said to the Hatter.

вҖңIt isnвҖҷt mine,вҖқ said the Hatter.

вҖңStolen!вҖқ the King exclaimed, turning to the jury, who instantly made
a memorandum of the fact.
11-5.prevIllustration: William H. Walker ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” In court

11-6.text:
вҖңI keep them to sell,вҖқ the Hatter added as an explanation; вҖңIвҖҷve none
of my own. IвҖҷm a hatter.вҖқ

Here the Queen put on her spectacles, and began staring at the Hatter,
who turned pale and fidgeted.

вҖңGive your evidence,вҖқ said the King; вҖңand donвҖҷt be nervous, or IвҖҷll
have you executed on the spot.вҖқ

This did not seem to encourage the witness at all: he kept shifting
from one foot to the other, looking uneasily at the Queen, and in his
confusion he bit a large piece out of his teacup instead of the
bread-and-butter.

Just at this moment Alice felt a very curious sensation, which puzzled
her a good deal until she made out what it was: she was beginning to
grow larger again, and she thought at first she would get up and leave
the court; but on second thoughts she decided to remain where she was
as long as there was room for her.
11-6.nextIllustration: Julia Margaret Cameron (photograph) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice with Cameron, 1872

11-7.text:
вҖңI wish you wouldnвҖҷt squeeze so,вҖқ said the Dormouse, who was sitting
next to her. вҖңI can hardly breathe.вҖқ

вҖңI canвҖҷt help it,вҖқ said Alice very meekly: вҖңIвҖҷm growing.вҖқ

вҖңYouвҖҷve no right to grow here,вҖқ said the Dormouse.

вҖңDonвҖҷt talk nonsense,вҖқ said Alice more boldly: вҖңyou know youвҖҷre growing
too.вҖқ

вҖңYes, but I grow at a reasonable pace,вҖқ said the Dormouse: вҖңnot in
that ridiculous fashion.вҖқ And he got up very sulkily and crossed over
to the other side of the court.

All this time the Queen had never left off staring at the Hatter, and,
just as the Dormouse crossed the court, she said to one of the officers
of the court, вҖңBring me the list of the singers in the last concert!вҖқ
on which the wretched Hatter trembled so, that he shook both his shoes
off.

вҖңGive your evidence,вҖқ the King repeated angrily, вҖңor IвҖҷll have you
executed, whether youвҖҷre nervous or not.вҖқ
11-7.illustrationDesc: Julia Margaret Cameron (photograph) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice with Cameron, 1872
11-7.nextIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The trial of the Knave of Hearts

11-8.text:
вҖңIвҖҷm a poor man, your Majesty,вҖқ the Hatter began, in a trembling voice,
вҖңвҖ”and I hadnвҖҷt begun my teaвҖ”not above a week or soвҖ”and what with the
bread-and-butter getting so thinвҖ”and the twinkling of the teaвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңThe twinkling of the what?вҖқ said the King.

вҖңIt began with the tea,вҖқ the Hatter replied.

вҖңOf course twinkling begins with a T!вҖқ said the King sharply. вҖңDo you
take me for a dunce? Go on!вҖқ

вҖңIвҖҷm a poor man,вҖқ the Hatter went on, вҖңand most things twinkled after
thatвҖ”only the March Hare saidвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңI didnвҖҷt!вҖқ the March Hare interrupted in a great hurry.

вҖңYou did!вҖқ said the Hatter.

вҖңI deny it!вҖқ said the March Hare.

вҖңHe denies it,вҖқ said the King: вҖңleave out that part.вҖқ

вҖңWell, at any rate, the Dormouse saidвҖ”вҖқ the Hatter went on, looking
anxiously round to see if he would deny it too: but the Dormouse denied
nothing, being fast asleep.
11-8.illustrationDesc: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The trial of the Knave of Hearts
11-8.prevIllustration: Julia Margaret Cameron (photograph) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice with Cameron, 1872

11-9.text:
вҖңAfter that,вҖқ continued the Hatter, вҖңI cut some more bread-and-butterвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңBut what did the Dormouse say?вҖқ one of the jury asked.

вҖңThat I canвҖҷt remember,вҖқ said the Hatter.

вҖңYou must remember,вҖқ remarked the King, вҖңor IвҖҷll have you executed.вҖқ

The miserable Hatter dropped his teacup and bread-and-butter, and went
down on one knee. вҖңIвҖҷm a poor man, your Majesty,вҖқ he began.

вҖңYouвҖҷre a very poor speaker,вҖқ said the King.

Here one of the guinea-pigs cheered, and was immediately suppressed by
the officers of the court. (As that is rather a hard word, I will just
explain to you how it was done. They had a large canvas bag, which tied
up at the mouth with strings: into this they slipped the guinea-pig,
head first, and then sat upon it.)

вҖңIвҖҷm glad IвҖҷve seen that done,вҖқ thought Alice. вҖңIвҖҷve so often read in
the newspapers, at the end of trials, вҖңThere was some attempts at
applause, which was immediately suppressed by the officers of the
court,вҖқ and I never understood what it meant till now.вҖқ
11-9.prevIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The trial of the Knave of Hearts

11-10.text:
вҖңIf thatвҖҷs all you know about it, you may stand down,вҖқ continued the
King. вҖңI canвҖҷt go no lower,вҖқ said the Hatter: вҖңIвҖҷm on the floor, as it is.вҖқ вҖңThen you may sit down,вҖқ the King replied. Here the other guinea-pig cheered, and was suppressed. вҖңCome, that finished the guinea-pigs!вҖқ thought Alice. вҖңNow we shall get
on better.вҖқ вҖңIвҖҷd rather finish my tea,вҖқ said the Hatter, with an anxious look at
the Queen, who was reading the list of singers. вҖңYou may go,вҖқ said the King, and the Hatter hurriedly left the court,
without even waiting to put his shoes on. вҖңвҖ”and just take his head off outside,вҖқ the Queen added to one of the
officers: but the Hatter was out of sight before the officer could get
to the door. вҖңCall the next witness!вҖқ said the King. The next witness was the DuchessвҖҷs cook. She carried the pepper-box in
her hand, and Alice guessed who it was, even before she got into the
court, by the way the people near the door began sneezing all at once. вҖңGive your evidence,вҖқ said the King.

11-11.text:
вҖңShanвҖҷt,вҖқ said the cook. The King looked anxiously at the White Rabbit, who said in a low voice,
вҖңYour Majesty must cross-examine this witness.вҖқ вҖңWell, if I must, I must,вҖқ the King said, with a melancholy air, and,
after folding his arms and frowning at the cook till his eyes were
nearly out of sight, he said in a deep voice, вҖңWhat are tarts made of?вҖқ вҖңPepper, mostly,вҖқ said the cook. вҖңTreacle,вҖқ said a sleepy voice behind her. вҖңCollar that Dormouse,вҖқ the Queen shrieked out. вҖңBehead that Dormouse!
Turn that Dormouse out of court! Suppress him! Pinch him! Off with his
whiskers!вҖқ For some minutes the whole court was in confusion, getting the Dormouse
turned out, and, by the time they had settled down again, the cook had
disappeared. вҖңNever mind!вҖқ said the King, with an air of great relief. вҖңCall the
next witness.вҖқ And he added in an undertone to the Queen, вҖңReally, my
dear, you must cross-examine the next witness. It quite makes my
forehead ache!вҖқ

Alice watched the White Rabbit as he fumbled over the list, feeling
very curious to see what the next witness would be like, вҖңвҖ”for they
havenвҖҷt got much evidence yet,вҖқ she said to herself. Imagine her
surprise, when the White Rabbit read out, at the top of his shrill
little voice, the name вҖңAlice!вҖқ

12-1.text:
вҖңHere!вҖқ cried Alice, quite forgetting in the flurry of the moment how
large she had grown in the last few minutes, and she jumped up in such
a hurry that she tipped over the jury-box with the edge of her skirt,
upsetting all the jurymen on to the heads of the crowd below, and there
they lay sprawling about, reminding her very much of a globe of
goldfish she had accidentally upset the week before.

вҖңOh, I beg your pardon!вҖқ she exclaimed in a tone of great dismay, and
began picking them up again as quickly as she could, for the accident
of the goldfish kept running in her head, and she had a vague sort of
idea that they must be collected at once and put back into the
jury-box, or they would die.

вҖңThe trial cannot proceed,вҖқ said the King in a very grave voice, вҖңuntil
all the jurymen are back in their proper placesвҖ”all,вҖқ he repeated
with great emphasis, looking hard at Alice as he said so.
12-1.nextIllustration: John Tenniel & Harry Theaker ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Tipped over jury box

12-2.text:
Alice looked at the jury-box, and saw that, in her haste, she had put
the Lizard in head downwards, and the poor little thing was waving its
tail about in a melancholy way, being quite unable to move. She soon
got it out again, and put it right; вҖңnot that it signifies much,вҖқ she
said to herself; вҖңI should think it would be quite as much use in the
trial one way up as the other.вҖқ

As soon as the jury had a little recovered from the shock of being
upset, and their slates and pencils had been found and handed back to
them, they set to work very diligently to write out a history of the
accident, all except the Lizard, who seemed too much overcome to do
anything but sit with its mouth open, gazing up into the roof of the
court.

вҖңWhat do you know about this business?вҖқ the King said to Alice.

вҖңNothing,вҖқ said Alice.

вҖңNothing whatever?вҖқ persisted the King.

вҖңNothing whatever,вҖқ said Alice.
12-2.illustrationDesc: John Tenniel & Harry Theaker ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Tipped over jury box
12-2.nextIllustration: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice & the Jurors

12-3.text:
вҖңThatвҖҷs very important,вҖқ the King said, turning to the jury. They were
just beginning to write this down on their slates, when the White
Rabbit interrupted: вҖңUnimportant, your Majesty means, of course,вҖқ he
said in a very respectful tone, but frowning and making faces at him as
he spoke.

вҖңUnimportant, of course, I meant,вҖқ the King hastily said, and went on
to himself in an undertone,

вҖңimportantвҖ”unimportantвҖ”unimportantвҖ”importantвҖ”вҖқ as if he were trying
which word sounded best.

Some of the jury wrote it down вҖңimportant,вҖқ and some вҖңunimportant.вҖқ Alice could see this, as she was near enough to look over their slates;
вҖңbut it doesnвҖҷt matter a bit,вҖқ she thought to herself.

At this moment the King, who had been for some time busily writing in
his note-book, cackled out вҖңSilence!вҖқ and read out from his book, вҖңRule
Forty-two. All persons more than a mile high to leave the court.вҖқ

Everybody looked at Alice.
12-3.illustrationDesc: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice & the Jurors
12-3.prevIllustration: John Tenniel & Harry Theaker ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Tipped over jury box

12-4.text:
вҖңIвҖҷm not a mile high,вҖқ said Alice.

вҖңYou are,вҖқ said the King.

вҖңNearly two miles high,вҖқ added the Queen.

вҖңWell, I shanвҖҷt go, at any rate,вҖқ said Alice: вҖңbesides, thatвҖҷs not a
regular rule: you invented it just now.вҖқ

вҖңItвҖҷs the oldest rule in the book,вҖқ said the King.

вҖңThen it ought to be Number One,вҖқ said Alice.

The King turned pale, and shut his note-book hastily. вҖңConsider your
verdict,вҖқ he said to the jury, in a low, trembling voice.

вҖңThereвҖҷs more evidence to come yet, please your Majesty,вҖқ said the
White Rabbit, jumping up in a great hurry; вҖңthis paper has just been
picked up.вҖқ

вҖңWhatвҖҷs in it?вҖқ said the Queen.

вҖңI havenвҖҷt opened it yet,вҖқ said the White Rabbit, вҖңbut it seems to be a
letter, written by the prisoner toвҖ”to somebody.вҖқ

вҖңIt must have been that,вҖқ said the King, вҖңunless it was written to
nobody, which isnвҖҷt usual, you know.вҖқ

вҖңWho is it directed to?вҖқ said one of the jurymen.
12-4.prevIllustration: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice & the Jurors

12-5.text:
вҖңIt isnвҖҷt directed at all,вҖқ said the White Rabbit; вҖңin fact, thereвҖҷs
nothing written on the outside.вҖқ He unfolded the paper as he spoke,
and added вҖңIt isnвҖҷt a letter, after all: itвҖҷs a set of verses.вҖқ

вҖңAre they in the prisonerвҖҷs handwriting?вҖқ asked another of the jurymen.

вҖңNo, theyвҖҷre not,вҖқ said the White Rabbit, вҖңand thatвҖҷs the queerest
thing about it.вҖқ (The jury all looked puzzled.)

вҖңHe must have imitated somebody elseвҖҷs hand,вҖқ said the King. (The jury
all brightened up again.)

вҖңPlease your Majesty,вҖқ said the Knave, вҖңI didnвҖҷt write it, and they
canвҖҷt prove I did: thereвҖҷs no name signed at the end.вҖқ

вҖңIf you didnвҖҷt sign it,вҖқ said the King, вҖңthat only makes the matter
worse. You must have meant some mischief, or else youвҖҷd have signed
your name like an honest man.вҖқ

There was a general clapping of hands at this: it was the first really
clever thing the King had said that day.
12-5.nextIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice growing in court, Off with her head

12-6.text:
вҖңThat proves his guilt,вҖқ said the Queen.

вҖңIt proves nothing of the sort!вҖқ said Alice. вҖңWhy, you donвҖҷt even know
what theyвҖҷre about!вҖқ

вҖңRead them,вҖқ said the King.

The White Rabbit put on his spectacles. вҖңWhere shall I begin, please
your Majesty?вҖқ he asked.

вҖңBegin at the beginning,вҖқ the King said gravely, вҖңand go on till you
come to the end: then stop.вҖқ

These were the verses the White Rabbit read:вҖ”

вҖңThey told me you had been to her,
    And mentioned me to him:
She gave me a good character,
    But said I could not swim.

He sent them word I had not gone
    (We know it to be true):
If she should push the matter on,
    What would become of you?

I gave her one, they gave him two,
    You gave us three or more;
They all returned from him to you,
    Though they were mine before.

If I or she should chance to be
    Involved in this affair,
He trusts to you to set them free,
    Exactly as we were.
12-6.illustrationDesc: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice growing in court, Off with her head
12-6.nextIllustration: Photograph ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice Hargreaves in 1932, age 80

12-7.text:
My notion was that you had been
    (Before she had this fit)
An obstacle that came between
    Him, and ourselves, and it.

DonвҖҷt let him know she liked them best,
    For this must ever be
A secret, kept from all the rest,
    Between yourself and me.вҖқ

вҖңThatвҖҷs the most important piece of evidence weвҖҷve heard yet,вҖқ said the
King, rubbing his hands; вҖңso now let the juryвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңIf any one of them can explain it,вҖқ said Alice, (she had grown so
large in the last few minutes that she wasnвҖҷt a bit afraid of
interrupting him,) вҖңIвҖҷll give him sixpence. I donвҖҷt believe thereвҖҷs
an atom of meaning in it.вҖқ

The jury all wrote down on their slates, вҖңShe doesnвҖҷt believe thereвҖҷs
an atom of meaning in it,вҖқ but none of them attempted to explain the
paper.
12-7.illustrationDesc: Photograph ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice Hargreaves in 1932, age 80
12-7.prevIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice growing in court, Off with her head
12-7.nextIllustration: Arthur Rackham ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Pack rose in the air

12-8.text:
вҖңIf thereвҖҷs no meaning in it,вҖқ said the King, вҖңthat saves a world of
trouble, you know, as we neednвҖҷt try to find any. And yet I donвҖҷt
know,вҖқ he went on, spreading out the verses on his knee, and looking at
them with one eye; вҖңI seem to see some meaning in them, after all.
вҖңвҖ”said I could not swimвҖ”вҖқ you canвҖҷt swim, can you?вҖқ he added, turning
to the Knave.

The Knave shook his head sadly. вҖңDo I look like it?вҖқ he said. (Which he
certainly did not, being made entirely of cardboard.)

вҖңAll right, so far,вҖқ said the King, and he went on muttering over the
verses to himself: вҖңвҖҳWe know it to be trueвҖ”вҖҷ thatвҖҷs the jury, of
courseвҖ”вҖҳI gave her one, they gave him twoвҖ”вҖҷ why, that must be what he
did with the tarts, you knowвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңBut, it goes on вҖҳthey all returned from him to you,вҖҷвҖқ said Alice.

вҖңWhy, there they are!вҖқ said the King triumphantly, pointing to the
tarts on the table. вҖңNothing can be clearer than that. Then
againвҖ”вҖҳbefore she had this fitвҖ”вҖҷ you never had fits, my dear, I
think?вҖқ he said to the Queen.
12-8.illustrationDesc: Arthur Rackham ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Pack rose in the air
12-8.prevIllustration: Photograph ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Alice Hargreaves in 1932, age 80
12-8.nextIllustration: Gwynedd Hudson ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Cards rose into the air

12-9.text:
вҖңNever!вҖқ said the Queen furiously, throwing an inkstand at the Lizard
as she spoke. (The unfortunate little Bill had left off writing on his
slate with one finger, as he found it made no mark; but he now hastily
began again, using the ink, that was trickling down his face, as long
as it lasted.)

вҖңThen the words donвҖҷt fit you,вҖқ said the King, looking round the
court with a smile. There was a dead silence.

вҖңItвҖҷs a pun!вҖқ the King added in an offended tone, and everybody
laughed, вҖңLet the jury consider their verdict,вҖқ the King said, for
about the twentieth time that day.

вҖңNo, no!вҖқ said the Queen. вҖңSentence firstвҖ”verdict afterwards.вҖқ

вҖңStuff and nonsense!вҖқ said Alice loudly. вҖңThe idea of having the
sentence first!вҖқ

вҖңHold your tongue!вҖқ said the Queen, turning purple.

вҖңI wonвҖҷt!вҖқ said Alice.

вҖңOff with her head!вҖқ the Queen shouted at the top of her voice. Nobody
moved.
12-9.illustrationDesc: Gwynedd Hudson ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Cards rose into the air
12-9.prevIllustration: Arthur Rackham ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Pack rose in the air
12-9.nextIllustration: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The Shower of Cards

12-10.text:
вҖңWho cares for you?вҖқ said Alice, (she had grown to her full size by
this time.) вҖңYouвҖҷre nothing but a pack of cards!вҖқ

At this the whole pack rose up into the air, and came flying down upon
her: she gave a little scream, half of fright and half of anger, and
tried to beat them off, and found herself lying on the bank, with her
head in the lap of her sister, who was gently brushing away some dead
leaves that had fluttered down from the trees upon her face.

вҖңWake up, Alice dear!вҖқ said her sister; вҖңWhy, what a long sleep youвҖҷve
had!вҖқ

вҖңOh, IвҖҷve had such a curious dream!вҖқ said Alice, and she told her
sister, as well as she could remember them, all these strange
Adventures of hers that you have just been reading about; and when she
had finished, her sister kissed her, and said, вҖңIt was a curious
dream, dear, certainly: but now run in to your tea; itвҖҷs getting late.вҖқ So Alice got up and ran off, thinking while she ran, as well she might,
what a wonderful dream it had been.
12-10.illustrationDesc: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The Shower of Cards
12-10.prevIllustration: Gwynedd Hudson ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” Cards rose into the air

12-11.text:
But her sister sat still just as she left her, leaning her head on her
hand, watching the setting sun, and thinking of little Alice and all
her wonderful Adventures, till she too began dreaming after a fashion,
and this was her dream:вҖ”

First, she dreamed of little Alice herself, and once again the tiny
hands were clasped upon her knee, and the bright eager eyes were
looking up into hersвҖ”she could hear the very tones of her voice, and
see that queer little toss of her head to keep back the wandering hair
that would always get into her eyesвҖ”and still as she listened, or
seemed to listen, the whole place around her became alive with the
strange creatures of her little sisterвҖҷs dream.
12-11.prevIllustration: John Tenniel (colorized E.G. Thomson) ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The Shower of Cards

12-12.text:
The long grass rustled at her feet as the White Rabbit hurried byвҖ”the
frightened Mouse splashed his way through the neighbouring poolвҖ”she
could hear the rattle of the teacups as the March Hare and his friends
shared their never-ending meal, and the shrill voice of the Queen
ordering off her unfortunate guests to executionвҖ”once more the pig-baby
was sneezing on the DuchessвҖҷs knee, while plates and dishes crashed
around itвҖ”once more the shriek of the Gryphon, the squeaking of the
LizardвҖҷs slate-pencil, and the choking of the suppressed guinea-pigs,
filled the air, mixed up with the distant sobs of the miserable Mock
Turtle.
12-12.nextIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The end - Alice waking up

12-13.text:
So she sat on, with closed eyes, and half believed herself in
Wonderland, though she knew she had but to open them again, and all
would change to dull realityвҖ”the grass would be only rustling in the
wind, and the pool rippling to the waving of the reedsвҖ”the rattling
teacups would change to tinkling sheep-bells, and the QueenвҖҷs shrill
cries to the voice of the shepherd boyвҖ”and the sneeze of the baby, the
shriek of the Gryphon, and all the other queer noises, would change
(she knew) to the confused clamour of the busy farm-yardвҖ”while the
lowing of the cattle in the distance would take the place of the Mock
TurtleвҖҷs heavy sobs.
12-13.illustrationDesc: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The end - Alice waking up

12-14.text:
Lastly, she pictured to herself how this same little sister of hers
would, in the after-time, be herself a grown woman; and how she would
keep, through all her riper years, the simple and loving heart of her
childhood: and how she would gather about her other little children,
and make their eyes bright and eager with many a strange tale,
perhaps even with the dream of Wonderland of long ago: and how she
would feel with all their simple sorrows, and find a pleasure in all
their simple joys, remembering her own child-life, and the happy summer
days.
12-14.prevIllustration: Lewis Carroll ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” The end - Alice waking up
